twitter_thread: 57% of retirement accounts hold fewer than 5 assets, missing out on $10k+ in potential annual returns.
Allocate 20% to dividend-paying stocks like JNJ ($3.80 dividend yield) and PG ($2.60 dividend yield) for steady income.
Holding 15 stocks instead of 5 can decrease returns by 3% without adding stability, according to a Vanguard study.
401(k) and Roth IRA accounts can save you $1,000+ in taxes annually, boosting your retirement savings.
REITs like Realty Income (8.2% average annual return) and National Retail Properties (7.5%) provide stable income streams.
Dollar-cost averaging with a $1,000 monthly investment can reduce risk by 15% over 10 years.
What's your favorite income-generating strategy and how has it performed for you? #incomestrategies #retirementplanning
